name: Connecticut DCP Major Contractor Lookup
updateFrequency: unknown; verify current status in Connecticut eLicense
knownExclusions: The major-contractor definition is limited to work exceeding statutory threshold limits or affecting structural integrity; it is not a universal general-contractor license.
Residential home improvement, new-home construction, occupational trades, asbestos credentials, and local permits are covered separately.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Do not automate the shared portal until its controls and public contract are reviewed.
publicRecordsNotes: Official source metadata only. A missing result is not proof that a contractor lacks another state or local authorization.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No stable, documented machine-readable major-contractor export has been confirmed.
researchNotes: DCP licenses major contractors for specified large or structurally significant work. The shared eLicense lookup remains a manual handoff.
verificationCaveats: Major-contractor coverage is narrower than all general or commercial construction.
No match means no matching record in this source at the checked time.
verificationNotes: No supported adapter is exposed for the shared interactive lookup.
maintainerNotes: Preserve the statutory threshold limitation in all future verification output.

researchEvidence.note: Official DCP page defines licensed major-contractor work and identifies roofing and structural work within scope.
Official shared public license lookup reviewed as the current verification handoff.

blocker.summary: The shared interactive lookup has no reviewed stable machine-readable contract for this credential.
